headline: France opens the sensitive issue of slavery reparations
description: Following a visit by President John Dramani Mahama to the Elysée, Ghana's foreign affairs minister announced on April 8 that Paris would be willing to 'collaborate' on 'restorative justice.' Those close to Emmanuel Macron confirmed that the French president supported the initiative.

headline: Iran reimposes restrictions on Strait of Hormuz, accusing US of violating deal to reopen it

France to allow bakers and florists to open on Labor Day under tight conditions
description: French Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu has announced a draft bill that will allow bakeries and florists to operate on May 1, provided employees volunteer and receive double pay. It could come into effect as soon as this year.

headline: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer once again weakened by the Mandelson affair
description: According to revelations by the 'Guardian' on Thursday, the former ambassador to the United States, implicated in the Jeffrey Epstein affair, was sent to Washington without having received the necessary security clearance. Keir Starmer said on Friday that he was stunned not to have been informed.

headline: French film star Nathalie Baye dead at 77
description: Baye, a stalwart of French cinema, starred in some 80 films and took home the Best Actress Cesar. She notably played Leonardo DiCaprio's mother in Steven Spielberg's 'Catch Me if You Can.'

headline: Can we detox our bodies from pollutants like 'forever chemicals'?
description: Scientists are testing methods to remove certain pollutants from the human body. These methods can be both invasive and costly, and do not prevent renewed exposure to the most widespread environmental toxins.

description: Despite major pressure on the power grid and the risk of rising greenhouse gas emissions, Dublin wants to keep building these facilities for tech giants and establish itself as a European leader in the sector.

headline: 'Romeo and Juliet,' choreographed by Rudolf Nureyev, returns to Paris
description: The spectacular production at the Opéra Bastille gives the new generation of dancers a chance to showcase their talents.

headline: 'Dansons,' Céline Dion's new single, already feels outdated
description: The superstar's new song, written by Jean-Jacques Goldman and launched with great fanfare on Friday, seems to belong to another era. It is far from the hits that the French singer-songwriter created for the Québécois diva in the 1990s.

headline: Vincent Bolloré's troubling grip on French publishing
'Why not try to remobilize a France that has faith in its future?'
'By firing a publisher, Bolloré united writers, who are usually unpredictable and solitary'
